DialogText: Child, I am a memory of Fernalla's final wish. You have proven yourself worthy of the task I will ask of you. Please, listen to me.

In my defeat at the hands of Sivakaya, her shadow found its way into me and clung to all that I am. It calls to me still, pulling me toward her endless dark. Towards her. In my last breaths, I used what light remained to give one final command, but I fear it has failed. I cannot find peace.

Help me find rest, child of the living world. Help me find peace. Together, we may gather the scattered fragments of my last moments and mend what has been broken. Tell me, have you found the resting place of my Dear Brother Vitheo?

He is interred near the sea, where Port Azure now stands. Beneath the city's streets. Though my nightmares have hinted there is no rest there at all. In his tomb's entry chamber, there is a guardian. Bring me a memory of his burial from it, and we might proceed.
My dear child, please... you have found the resting place of my brother Vitheo. I feel his presence, faint as the tide against a distant shore, yet I cannot remember why it calls to me so.
Balaste, his dearest friend Balaste, was to watch over him and then to be laid to rest near him. I remember asking so much of him in my final moments... though I do not know if he kept his vigil.
Please, seek his remains. See if you can feel what I no longer remember... a memory of what transpired there. Bring it to me, so that I may know the truth. *A key materializes in your hand*
Elwio... I feel great regret for him. I cannot recall why but it was to protect Vitheo from Sivakaya's darkness, for Vitheo was her son... She would have never let him out of her dark grip.

Elwio did a great favor for Vitheo and for me. I must recall it, I must know why I feel such sadness, so that I can try to heal and free the strength to lift this darkness off of me. Please, find Elwio. *A key materializes in your hand*
Please, Elwio may hold the memories I cannot recall any longer. If you can find him, help him to rest and see what feelings you can recall on his behalf.
I saw it, it's true, and I wrote it down, and now they took it, and I NEED IT! I have to tell someone...
They took it! My toolbox. It's not tools though, it's not. It's everything else. The highwaymen... across the field they went, one has it...
RepeatingQuestDialog: I've told you this already!

DialogText: I saw it, it's true, and I wrote it down, and now they took it, and I NEED IT! I have to tell someone...
They took it! My toolbox. It's not tools though, it's not. It's everything else. The highwaymen... across the field they went, one has it...
Keywords: What, take, who, highwaymen, toolbox, tool, box

DialogText: ... *a memory can be felt in the air and a low voice echoes through your mind* - "The guardian has awoken, let her gaze upon you"...
RepeatingQuestDialog: Let her gaze upon you.

DialogText: ... *a memory can be felt in the air and a low voice echoes through your mind* - "Only the truly pious shall pass. He who speaks the name of the one who gifted Sivakaya her ascension may pass unharmed."
Heh... another one.
A swine. A demon. A trickster.
Umbran....? Yes... I remember. Yes... you are one of us. The remnants. You will help to remove the demon. To restore the garden. Bring the riftsnare. Pull the souls into our realm.
RepeatingQuestDialog: The Riftsnares... pull the souls into this realm! Remove all four to reveal the weakness of the demon!

DialogText: Heh... his power flows through this place. Replaced by another but never absent.
Yesss!!! I feel it! The POWER!

I cannot fathom what may lay in the heart of that place. My men do not have the ability to defeat its protectors. Vitheos own sword might be within those depths, waiting for able hands to swing it once again.
Ancient Vithean spirits and corrupted protectors. They present no resemblance to their former glory, but they're as deadly as they ever were.
I feel safer near the guards. There's just no telling... I've heard odd things in the housing pit late at night.
We can fight if we have to... but we hope we don't. We're separating ourselves from Port Azure. We're not here to battle the Sivakayans. I think they know that...
Yarren? Yeah, he's around here somewhere. Haven't seen him in a day or so.
I don't go out much anymore. I can't stay here.
Yarren? Yeah, he's around here somewhere. Haven't seen him in a day or so.
Not often a new face travels past this place. Aside from the unwelcoming Faeries and those old Portal Ruins, there wasn't much here to be seen... not until recently at least.
Ripper and her crew took hold of the Fortress a few weeks back. The Sivakayans were pushed out - which is benefical to all of us if you ask me.
Her motives aren't clear, and it is not my place to involve myself. I stand watch at this Willow, and that is my only concern.
One of Fernalla's seeds fell here. This Willow is a holy place, and as such it will always be acoompanied by one of her faithful children.  It is my turn to stand watch.
Nothing much to be done now. I'm more than able to help defend this place if need be, but I don't plan to seek out the coming battle before its due.
Name's Akaran, but they call me Mule 'round here. Means I got stuff to sell. You need some gear?
It's because I've always got the goods, pal.
Name's Akaran, but they call me Mule 'round here. Means I got stuff to sell. You need some gear?
It's because I've always got the goods, pal.
We're trying to make our way here. It's not easy. At least we've got Ripper. Lost most everything else.
If anyone says anything bad about Ripper, don't believe them. You'll hear all sorts of lies in Port Azure.

It's all gone for me. My wife is missing. Has been for some time now.  I'm terrified that she's been raised from the dead by the Sivakayans. If you find her... if she has her ring... please bring it to me so I know she's at rest finally.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thank you again for helping me.

DialogText: Sometimes I hear folks talkin' about ghosts that only show up at night. One specific one they all mention, over on the West Vithean Coastline, up by the Malaroths. Not gonna go and see for myself, but I heard 'bout it.
The watchlights... have you seen them? They're all over the island. What do you think they're for?
They've been here for ages. They seem harmless, but it's unnerving!
Fernalla did not emerge from the gate during the raid of Goodsoil...  Everybody expected her to return. Maybe they were truly unbelievers.
Goodsoil? Surely you've seen the plume of smoke in the distance. That was Goodsoil.
The gate is just over the hill. It's said that there's a spirit of the willow that can open it if you know how to ask. It would never speak to non-believers.

Only those who embrace the gift of life and the necessity of death are true believers. You'll find some who worship Fernalla only for her gifts, while denying that all gifts must one day be returned. True believers - and especially druids - embrace all parts of nature.
Fernalla did not emerge from the gate during the raid of Goodsoil...  Everybody expected her to return. Maybe they were truly unbelievers.
Pretty sure I'm moving back to the city soon. The Molorai aren't the most pleasant bunch to be around.
They just descended on this place a few days ago. Seem hellbent on getting into the ruins. Saw some walkin' around behind that old school down there too, like they know something about it we don't.
Have you found your aura yet? These gems will help. Even those near you will enjoy the benefits. Be sure to select one that you feel a connection with.
Yes! Companions who share your adventures will all benefit from the power of your aura, and you'll also benefit from theirs. Take one, try it!
A Croc jumped right out of the water and I got so scared I dropped my necklace... and he ATE IT! He went off towards the lighthouse... if you get it back I'll be so grateful!
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thanks again!

DialogText: It's right over there... the lighthouse is just past the office.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thanks for getting it back!

DialogText: He ate my necklace and ran off...
Alitho, collector of rarities and fine things, at your service!
Yes! All things! Lately though, I've been looking for Rottenfoot Sashes. If you find some, bring them to me!
Savages from the swamp up north. There's no sense in asking nicely, if you get my drift. The swamp is just about the last place you'll find any civilized folks.
Welcome to our little settlement. We're all fellow adventurers here. Take a look at what I've got for sale... I'm sure it would be useful for you. I could also use a favor.

There's a place just to the north tucked into the cliffside called 'The Bonepits.' It's full of skeletons and probably some necromancers. I need a skeleton eye... it's rare to find one on a dead skeleton, but they do exist.  Bring me one, and I've got something for you in return.
AssignQuestStableKey: quest:a skeleton eye for alyssa fearnon

DialogText:
Have you found your aura yet? These gems will help. Even those near you will enjoy the benefits. Be sure to select one that you feel a connection with.
(Right click to use a Vendor)
Yes! Companions who share your adventures will all benefit from the power of your aura, and you'll also benefit from theirs. Take one, try it!
Ah, now you've met the only person not trying to leave this place. Did my time as an adventurer.  Did it all, really. Now this quiet life is the life for me. Even sellin' some of my old stuff.
I've done it all. Seen it all. Slayed it all. I don't do the merc stuff anymore, but I do odd jobs.  I've even worked the docks.

Beast of Stowaway's Step they call him. Seen him chewing on all kinds of old trinkets. Probably there's somethin' valuable in his gut. Don't go pokin' at him without some muscle though, else you'll be in that gut yourself.
Instruction is key to your success, do not miss your lessons - we have no space for those who do not apply themselves.
Find your way to class, pupil. We do not tolerate daydreamers.
I barely got away... Goodsoil, it's gone...
I don't know... I can't... I don't... why us? Where was Fernalla?

Good riddance to Yarren if he's truly gone. I'm tired of hearing his views on the Sivakayans. He's probably off with those other apologists down in Restful Valley... by the swamps where they all belong.
Do not disrespect the Shrine to Vitheo, Lord of Air and Sea. He brings us the wind to sail, and the sea to traverse. He brings us this island to call home.
There is no way to Erenshor from here. The ports are closed and no sailor in his right mind would risk the consequences of that trip.
Vitheo is Lord of Air and Sea. His skill with all weapons is legendary, and those who follow him long to be as effective with a weapon as he, though none ever will be.
Heya! What brings ya to The Strand? Quite a place, it is. Claimed my little corner here. Self sufficient, I am! And an inventor too!

I can make anything outta anything! Know what I'm most interested in though? Some of those Nagalok claws. Ugly monsters, them. Moved in up the hill a while back and leave me alone mostly, but I betcha I could make a thing or two from those claws. Bring me a few - say... 5? You can keep whatever it is i whip up.
Those ugly things right up the hill there by the rune stones. Go mix it up with a few and bring me their claws. They're aggressive though... watch it!

Well hello there. Welcome to the Stormcaller's little corner of Azure. We had to set up ouside of the walls, can't be firing off arrows for target practice inside the city. You need anything, we're here.

We can share our knowledge and teach you skills you'd only dream of. We're here on Erenshor hoping to find lost knowledge, and to sharpen our abilities ever more. Do not be a stranger among us, and may the winds of Erenshor guide you to better things.
How did you enter this place? Azynthi has sealed me here... and cursed me to stay. I cannot leave even if this place crumbles.
He is everywhere, he is ascending. He stole the power of Sivakaya and grows with each moment into something truly terrifying.
If you can restore me, even if I cannot leave this place, I will bestow my knowledge upon you once I regain it.

He feeds on all who are here. My knowledge... he has taken it. I am one of the first Paladins. I escaped Astra, and I have seen Soluna's Celestial Blade. Azynthi has taken all but remnants of the memory from me.
Be a shame if I had to hurt you.
Be a shame if I had to hurt you.
The importance of this place cannot be overstated.
The importance of this place cannot be overstated.
Do not linger near me. I am only here to protect the discovery.
Do not linger near me. I am only here to protect the discovery.
I'm not the one you should speak to.
I'm not the one you should speak to.
I am Arabetha, emissary for Eldoth Molorai. You may conduct any official business through me.
Eldoth is a world renowned antiquities dealer and Erenshor's greatest advocate for preservation of history. With his help, we might deter Port Azure from pillaging this land for financial gain.
They are interlopers. A pestilence upon this land. Do not consort with them. Do not conduct business there, or you too will need to be cleansed by Eldoth.

Those here to benefit financially or those who will defile history for the sake of 'adventure' are not welcome. It is our sacred right to destroy any who set foot on historic ground within Erenshor. You included.

Your reception within Erenshor's ruins will only be warmed by your escaping blood. Eldoth will be pleased with our work. Eldoth is the only one who is able to channel Erenshor's power into something wonderful.
I long to return to the Sea but they have made it impossible.
Souls of the departed. The Watchlights. Their final revenge. Should the night come when all of them are extinguished, my release may be upon me.

Long ago I dispatched a band of Warlocks. In their final breaths they cursed me to remain here, this place that I protected with such dedication. They anchored their souls to this island where they fell - they are the watchlights. There are four.

Stalzo, Revid, Leah, and Posta. Their names echo eternally through my mind. Each has hidden a soul gem on the island. Force the soul gem upon its corresponding watchlight, and the light will be extinguished and its soul captured. If you can bring me their souls, I will be indebted to you beyond measure.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Ah, gloating I see, yes! They were four, now they are none!

DialogText: I have reason to believe many of the soul gems are in the island tomb. Guarded by the remains of those necromancers. There is a small sanctuary deep within - a stone throne and pews adorn the room. Behind the throne, the wall is false. It's a disguised door. Open it, and look there for clues.
Yes... their presence is still here. Do not believe their wicked lies if they can spit them before you remove their tongues. Those necromancers are devilish, devious things. Do not speak to them!
They will claim that I am their captive here. Imprisoned... a demon. Do not believe them. Help me be free of their curse!
Not many folks are tough enough for the swamp. Port Azure doesn't support us here. We're on our own past this point. Some of us like it that way.
If the rottenfoots don't get ya, the wildlife probably will. There's rumors of some pretty fantastic beasts out there... not to mention Sivakayans.
Not many folks are tough enough for the swamp. Port Azure doesn't support us here. We're on our own past this point. Some of us like it that way.
If the rottenfoots don't get ya, the wildlife probably will. There's rumors of some pretty fantastic beasts out there... not to mention Sivakayans... and the wyrms. Watch for those.
Some meetings are not for all ears. You'd be best off if you excused yourself.
By the will of the wisp, we shall know only greatness.
You... are not like Us.... We are... no more?
I sense our time has long past. I sense you do not belong, yet here you are.
Brax and Siva - the creators... the lovers... I feel tragedy. Who has been made to suffer so greatly?

She has been warped and hurt! I can see it. By... Azynthi? Her star pupil? Now he hides amongst your kind!  If you find him, you must gain power to defeat him! Here, bring this to one who knows its worth, and who mourns her loss. They will know what to do with it.
Sivakaya and Brax are the creators and we are fortunate to learn their ways.
Do not distract me! I do not have time for games.
Are you here to learn about Brax or about Sivakaya?
May the world always allow for us to learn together in peace...
Howdy. Welcome to Ripper's Keep.
Home away from home now. We've made our choice.
Don't ask. It's not a pretty story.

Killed our own brothers in Port Azure when they turned their back on the people. Broke free of that place and came here. This is our home now, and anyone who's willing to join is offered a path to do it. Go see Ripper.
Ah, got some rare wares here ya know. See if anything tickles ya.
A willow still grows here, and here, I will stand vigil for Fernalla. This place is not what it once was.

At the crest of that hill you'll begin to see it. It does not waver and from it, the blight eminates through all living things. The corpses of the people caught in Brax's holy fire are walking about, and the forest beyond the spire is full of even worse things.

Brax burned this desert into the land to stop the blight. This is the place where Sivakaya accepted her gift of necromancy. On that day a Goddess was corrupted, and the energy materialized itself into that dreadful spire.
A willow still grows here, and here, I will stand vigil for Fernalla. This place is not what it once was.

At the crest of that hill you'll begin to see it. It does not waver and from it, the blight eminates through all living things. The corpses of the people caught in Brax's holy fire are walking about, and the forest beyond the spire is full of even worse things.

Brax burned this desert into the land to stop the blight. This is the place where Sivakaya accepted her gift of necromancy. On that day a Goddess was corrupted, and the energy materialized itself into that dreadful spire.
Shame about Goodsoil. The others will be concerned.
You're known to The Wisp, after what you did on Stowaway's Step. Come see us in Loomingwood if you haven't already.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: It's to the north, not an easy walk and once you're there, don't stray from the road. We've warded it against the Sivakayans, but the wards have attacted some other things.
Do not cause trouble, citizen. The Azure Guard is entitled to your life.
Do not cause trouble, citizen. The Azure Guard is entitled to your life.
Do not cause trouble, citizen. The Azure Guard is entitled to your life.
What a tragedy...
The entire town has been destroyed by... by who knows what...
Who?
These folks have been through enough. Do not stir trouble.
I'm not authorized to answer any questions.
This is the territory of Port Azure. You are subject to the city laws beyond if you head into town. Just so you know... the laws are not for your protection - they are for the protection of the city.
It's not advised to adventure to the north. Go at your own risk, just stay out of the ruins. Everyone knows that... but you don't look too bright.
Oi, who are you? You the one I'm taking?
Yeah. Ok. Get in the boat. Say nothin' to noone.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Yeah. Ok. Get in the boat. Say nothin' to noone.

DialogText: I have been spared for the time being, but there's trouble in this place.
Pilgrims from Port Azure settled here, but when Port Azure pulled back its forces, the Sivakayans emerged and destroyed everything. A lot of people were not fortunate enough to escape in time.
Seen 'em all over the place up here. Makes me miss the walls of Port Azure. Getting back there is a journey I'm not sure I have in me anymore. There's others here too besides the Sivakayans...

Well, there's Ripper's soldiers who stay around to protect us after Port Azure abandoned us. Then there's that other group... always dressed in dark clothes and keeps to themselves mostly. Don't know much about them.
He and his men broke off from Port Azure when Port Azure pulled back. They're all that stands between us and the Sivakayans now. They're incredible in combat - I've seen it!
Welcome to Port Azure! The greatest place in Erenshor. You can get anything you need in this place!
Welcome to Port Azure! The greatest place in Erenshor. You can get anything you need in this place!
Thank you for everything you've done here. By recreating the happiest parts of our lives, you've brought us home.
Not often a traveler comes through. Not with all the goings-on 'round here. It's all a big turn off to folks.
It's a long list. Sivakayans, the Molorai troublemakers, Goodsoil... the abandonment of all the settlers...
Little town just outside of the Brake. Rumor has it Sivakayans ransacked it. Burnt it right to the ground, even. You seen it? I've only heard...

They're everywhere all of a sudden. Folks keep saying they seen squads of 'em headed here to Port Azure. None to be seen in town though. Makes you wonder if there's some hidden way in... if they're runnin' 'round inside the walls...

Eldoth Molorai's no stranger to town. Showed up a year ago maybe. Brought a shipload of brutes with him. They set up their own camp, rumor has it one of 'em is old Krakengard over on Vitheo's beach. They cause toruble here and there. Old Eldoth is involved in a lot more than meets the eye, I suspect.

Port Azure pulled back all its soldiers. Every last one of 'em. Lots of settlers too stubborn to come too though. Goodsoil paid the price. There's no protection outside of the city now. Folks are on their own. Makes you wonder what we don't know about the Sivakayans...

Benson Gamble, Knight of Soluna and master Paladin at your service. I have a lot that could benefit you, if you'd like to donate to my cause. Wanna prove yourself as a Paladin? I've got a job for you if you do.
... that means if you'd like to buy something.

I thought maybe you would. You seen the bonepits yet? Its a dark little cave to the north there. Go clear it out for a while. You'll probably happen upon some Graveweed Leaf. Putrid stuff, that. Bring me some if you find it.
All are welcome here, but for any civilian who doesn't follow Soluna, the journey isn't exactly worth while.

This is the place where Soluna landed long ago, and bestowed her gift upon the world when she created The First Paladins. She then walked to that hilltop, and re-ascended to her plane. Her guardians remain behind, protecting this holy place from... from something. I dread to know what.
Passage to the Garden is limited to those with a boatpass. Show me yours to proceed.
Beautiful place. In good hands, these days.
We can return at your whim. Step onto the boat.
I just come out here for the fishing. Port Azure says don't venture this far into the wilds... but here I am.
That army marched through here not long ago... right to the hilltop they went. Tents on their backs, like they plan to stay a while.
Don't know who they were. Defintely marched with a purpose though.
Sorcerers can manipulate these portals. Maybe someday, you'll learn to do it too.
Well met, traveller. If you want to know more about this contraption behind me, just ask.
Quite a place, this field. Really one of the last places you'll see civilized folk trying to settle. Venturing past here never was a good idea. Apparently, being here wasn't a great idea either.
Port Azure Guards used to patrol through 'ere, up to Rottenfoot. Beyond that, folks was always on their own. A few pushed on to settle in the North, but they're not the average bunch.
You heard about Goodsoil? Gone overnight. Sivakayans, I'm betting.
Brax chose to sacrifice his own people when he scorched this desert onto the land. The desert holds back the blight, and the sacrifice was necessary.
It's some sort of a game! Some ancient passtime... but being down there makes me so uneasy.
We have reason to believe that the lost people of this place may exist beneath our very feet.

Our lost ancestors, buried by these holy sands sowed by Brax Himself to stop the blight from consuming the world. The writings we've uncovered allude to warnings given by Brax, and hint at a network of underground tunnels meant to sustain life.

The spire to the east sprung from the ground centuries ago and with it came decay and disease. It spread rapidly through the land until Brax scorched this place with a flourish of His hand. In doing so, he sacrificed his own followers. It is our duty to uncover their story, and honor them in history.

This grate has proved impenetrable, but something lies below. A constant wind blows from it. The air smells of Brashell... Brashell was thought to be extinct - it is an herb used in Braxonian rituals long ago.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Yes, so it was Brashell we smelled! Amazing!

DialogText: We've been unable to move it. If you can find a way in... the things you may find may change our world forever. Please, you look like the type to try. If you do, keep us apprised of your findings.
Have you seen it? We've uncovered something incredible... and the energy down there - you can FEEL it wanting for something!
The ground has been hardened into stone in many places, we are having trouble excavating the city.
All we have to do is find one opening, one window or rooftop hatch, and we may gain entry to centuries of history!
In old Braxonian, it's called 'Evadne' so it would seem - as that word is written in every text that references the entrance to Braxonia.
This place is incredible! There's no doubt that fallen Braxonia lies beneath our feet... the question is: how much of it is still there?

The prized city of Brax, it's said that he graced it with his own presence and blessed it with a portal to his own plane of existence. When the corruption came, he had to bury it in sand and fire to save the rest of the world.
Not a lot is known yet... Sivakaya fell in this place, and the spire of corruption rose from the sands. The remnants of that event still walk the sands here, tread with care.
There are strange ores to be found in this place called Planar Stones. Have you seen any? A whole one is exceedingly rare!
Their origin is not known, but there is nothing else like them. Somehow, they're tied to a blessing of the Gods, but we cannot figure out exactly how.
If you find an item that carries a blessing, it'll have a certain... sparkle to it. I believe that can strip its magic though! I've only seen it done once.
They're special things. With a magical resolving fluid in place of a mold, and if we combine 8 of the shard pieces with heat, I have reason to believe we can create a full and intact Planar Stone!

All we need is a blessed item, and we use an Inert Diamond in place of a mold. Then we apply heat and the diamond will vibrate and it will crumble, but its shards will change... they will absorb the magic from the item, leaving the physical item intact even!
An entire ancient civilization was buried under the sand. We are working to uncover its secrets.
Those ruins near the sea appear to have been some sort of a school of magic. The male statue is the image of Brax, and we cannot be certain but it would seem the female is Sivakayan in some form.

She is written about as an Elder Goddess of Creation, alongside Brax. Something went wrong, and she became corrupted. Erenshor is surely the place where that event happened - we're uncovering  evidence at every turn.
He and Sivakaya worked hand in hand to create this world - when Sivakaya became corrupted they could no longer co-exist.

It must have been an incredible place. Now, it sits empty. We've scoured it but found nothing - but at night we cannot tread there, for it comes back to life and its centuries of conflict make themselves known. If you come across anything, please show me.
That old school holds secrets, but we cannot find them. It changes at night and becomes dangerous... I wonder if it would reveal something else to someone who could navigate it after dark?
Spirits walk those stones, ethereal remnants of pupils and teachers it would seem. They are wildly aggressive to intruders, we've lost brothers attempting to communicate with them.
Our brotherhood has run this lighthouse since the day it was constructed. The flame never dies, by Brax's will.
The Braxon Brotherhood is well versed in many things, though our specialty is Arcanism.
Brax's flames left the inhabitants of this place cursed to wander as undead. Be cautious in the lower desert if you're treasure hunting.
Some of our best findings regarding Brax have come from our dig sites here.

What lies beyond this point are horrors who answer to no god. Their souls are blighted and their bodies are no longer their own. The artifacts you may find are not worth the toll this place will take.
This is an exciting time for us. We're close to uncovering the secrets of the blighted spire.
Every new dig site reveals something more curious than the last. This though... this find seems different.
May Brax smile upon us all on this day!
I make furniture. Infuse it with a bit o' magic here and there too. If you find blueprints, bring them to me and I can make them!
Weapons for sale. Need any?
In this light, it's a nice quiet place. Don't you think so? The bridge is wonderful, but only if you can see it!
RepeatingQuestDialog: In this light, it's a nice quiet place. Don't you think so?

DialogText: Heh, you've got it!
Ah, hello you! You're new here.
Can't get nothin' to grow up on these rocks. This moss is everywhere but nothing else. Wish I could get to that shrine and try there...

Legends has it Fernalla's orphaned followers took soil from beneath a Willow and tried to plant one here. Didn't work, 'cause i don't see no Willow. I bet the soil is good though. Look at the weeds growin' in it. That tiny giant decided he was gonna live there though. Can't get close to 'im.

Never seen one so small but no doubt it's some sorta giant offspring. Been that size for years, so I'm told. Dangerous as any of 'em. If someone removes 'im and shows me proof so I don't get snuck up on by 'im later on, I'll make it worth their while.
Who... who are you? We are so thankful to you... I would never have expected that I'd see a happy ending.
Preppin' to depart. Not to Erenshor though - those ports are all closed. I'm headed back home to Merosavilla for a little rest.
No can do. It'd take quite a tidy sum for me to risk my ship on a trip to Erenshor.

Merosavilla? That's where my home is. Nice place - lots of mountains. You should visit it someday. If I were going back to Ashenvale, I'd offer you a trip back since you didn't make it to Erenshor. You'll find your way, I'm sure.
You're new here. Keep in the light. Sivakayans are lurking somewhere and Ripper's on the prowl in the North.
If it aint them it's the Molorai or the Priel crew. They're both small potatoes though. Sivakayans are our threat right now. If you catch wind of anything, let me know.
A Traitor. Defied orders and defected with a legion of troops. Be careful of them if you happen upon any. Not to be trusted. For any person who slays Ripper, I have a reward.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You did good work. Ripper was not an easy opponent, I am sure.

DialogText: You looking for Ripper?
Go Upstairs. If you're looking to join up, Ripper's who you need.
We need all the help we can get. Between Port Azure and Kilkay... and I'm sure the Sivakayan's aren't happy that we took their keep... we don't have many friends up here.
Must have been 3 dozen of them in here when we arrived. Quite a fight, it was. We didn't lose a single man. They turned tail and ran off.
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
They're making us dig... they won't tell us what for. Please help us!
I feel safer near the guards. There's just no telling... I've heard odd things in the housing pit late at night.
I'm afraid I can't be much help to you. I'm just a writer. I could give you directions, I suppose, if you wanted me to.

Port Azure is just down the road past the next house. Across the pond along the road, that's Fernalla's Revival Plains.  If you take a right before you get to town, you'll find Vitheo's Watch and an old abandoned fort called Krakengard.
Wow, I don't get asked to elaborate on that very often! I write about lost treasures. I study maps, can usually put 'em together and find their secrets pretty quickly.
I'm afraid I can't be much help to you. I'm just a writer. I could give you directions, I suppose, if you wanted me to.

Port Azure is just down the road past the next house. Across the pond along the road, that's Fernalla's Revival Plains. and If you take a right before you get to town, you'll find Vitheo's Watch and an old abandoned Fort called Krakengard.
Odd, the portraits on the wall here do not depict Sivakaya...
I can only imagine that the male is Azynthi, but who is she?
It seems as though these Sivakayan soldiers may have allegiance to someone besides Sivakaya.
Azynthi may have somehow taken Sivakaya's power and now he's puppeting her, to direct her followers to his own legions.
For Brax, brother. You've found us! The brotherhood of Braxtons has given us quarter, and it is from here we will launch our expeditions!
There are artifacts in other worlds that were not meant for human hands, and by the gods we'll prove we can manage them! Our hunt began centuries ago and we are close now!
They follow the ways of Brax just as we Arcanists do. They just do it for less... material reasons. They still offer a lot of knowledge that we all can benefit from.
The keys to the ethereal planes are not easy to find, but rumors of their existences do grace the winds. We must be diligent.
For Brax, brother. You've found us! The brotherhood of Braxtons has given us quarter, and it is from here we will launch our expeditions!
There are artifacts in other worlds that were not meant for human hands, and by the gods we'll prove we can manage them! Our hunt began centuries ago and we are close now!
They follow the ways of Brax just as we Arcanists do. They just do it for less... material reasons. They still offer a lot of knowledge that we all can benefit from.
The keys to the ethereal planes are not easy to find, but rumors of their existences do grace the winds. We must be diligent.
I am Cerynth, eyes of Eldoth. Any business you conduct here will be noted. Eldoth does not allow the trading of Erenshor's antiquities to go unpunished.
He is the one who will bring Erenshor back to its glory and he will host the Gods themselves, welcoming them back to this land through his own body and being.
Erenshor is a land steeped in loss and tragedy. Eldoth will allow that to go on no longer. The ignorance of Port Azure and its population is an insult to the history of this place.

He will allow them to exist through himself. He will be a being supreme to any and benevolent to all who follow him. The rest will be extinguished. You and the rest of these interlopers are enemies of Eldoth, and you should not continue down that road.
You are already guilty. Do not tread upon Erenshor's places of significance, lest you tread no more.
So you're the one who's tagging along? Fine. Not a word of what you've seen though, or else you'll be a topic we revisit. Are you ready to go?
Good. The ship is coming soon. Jump in one of those boats and we'll row out to meet it. We'll drop you away from the city so we don't raise suspicion around you. Then we're square.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: She who gives life must also bring death, it is the way of nature and of Fernalla. Her wrath has recently been felt here.

This place is Fernalla's Revival Plains. Long ago, when Fernalla ascended to her Godly position, she did so right in this field. Her followers built her a gate to an alter plane, and she passed through to rule from atop the Great Willow where she can see all and know everything.

A Willow is a symbol of Fernalla. The one that grows here in the plains is the largest on Erenshor. It's said that her mortal body is buried beneath it. It grows on top of small cavern, full of gorgeous plants. You can see it yourself, just by the tree there.
You don't have time for me. Get on with ya.
You don't have time for me. Get on with ya.
I don't go out anymore. There's just too much danger out there. I wish I never came to this place.
To Erenshor. It was supposed to be a land of new opportunities, but here I am locked in my house in a city, too afraid to take advantage of any of it.
You! You there! Help me!
They've taken me prisoner! I've told them nothing but they know that I know things. Open this cage for me! Please!
Yes! Thank you! You've done both of us a favor just now. I have friends... powerful friends. Go see them hidden within walls of the Port Azure docks. They'll want a passphrase - it's "Shadowtide".
You! You there! Help me!
They've taken me prisoner! I've told them nothing but they know that I know things. Open this cage for me! Please!
Yes! Thank you! You've done both of us a favor just now. I have friends... powerful friends. Go see them hidden within walls of the Port Azure docks. They'll want a passphrase - it's "Shadowtide".
Peaceful out here. The wildlife doesn't come to the shoreline. Gets me away from the politics of Port Azure.
Ah, another Child of Soluna making The Pilgrimage! A fantastic journey it has been, has it not? Stop and rest for a spell, if you'd like.

The very site where Soluna once landed upon this plane is just up the hill. Her royal guard still stands watch for her return and the remaining energy of her presence there is nothing less than all-consuming.
Ah, another Child of Soluna making The Pilgrimage! A fantastic journey it has been, has it not? Stop and rest for a spell, if you'd like.

The very site where Soluna once landed upon this plane is just up the hill. Her royal guard still stands watch for her return and the remaining energy of her presence there is nothing less than all-consuming.
I've got connections in Azure. I can keep things going indefinitely, trust me.

Welcome to Stowaway's Step. If you're here to turn in dock papers, please do it now. If you don't have them, go back to the docks and get them. If you've already turned them in, move along. I can't keep track of everyone! And please... don't ask about going to Erenshor. You can't.
Yeah, you're registered. You can head up the hill into Wickliff. Go see Slate up at the gate. He'll get you a key to get through.

Not happenin' pal. The mainland has enough of its own problems right now without us funneling new adventurers in to stir up all the sleeping dungeons and caves hunting for treasures. If you hear of anyone offering to get you there, you come and tell me.
Old Kio's on his way out. Nobody's been in that lighthouse since he took over. Lost his mind doing that job.  It's sad. I've got an extra key in case he doesn't... cooperate.
Whatcha want?
And what? You want your own key to this door? Got no more on me. You wanna take care of a problem for me? Doin' it comes with its own key.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Is that problem taken care of?

DialogText: It's a big problem. Kio - that guy down at the Lighthouse there - he's been making folks uncomfortable. We're gonna send him on leave for a bit. Go and deliver the news and collect his gate key. It's all yours.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You already took care of this.

DialogText: The nightmares are too much. It's the light. Kio keeps it that way.  The guardians of this place do not like change.
There are six... The Walking Dream, The Ghost of the Great Beast, The Shadowy Spider, The Scourge at the Cliffside Ruins, Mephallo in the Sky, and.... Him.
Fresh food for sale here, buy some!
Fresh food for sale here, buy some!
The grass does need a good trim, doesn't it. Could be anything hiding in there. He likes to hide in there. Won't let me trim it. Says it's nice in this light.
The grass does need a good trim, doesn't it? Could be anything hiding in there. He likes to hide in there. Won't let me trim it. Says it's nice in this light.
RepeatingQuestDialog: The grass does need a good trim, doesn't it. Could be anything hiding in there. He likes to hide in there. Won't let me trim it. Says it's nice in this light.

DialogText: Welcome to Stowaway Step, stranger. Please go check in at the office up ahead. Here is your dock pass. Give it to the agent inside. Do not lose it!
RepeatingQuestDialog: You've already registered... get off my docks.

DialogText: Yes, the big building just to the right of the dock here. See Wendyl inside with your papers to register.
This isn't the life any of us had planned when we came to Erenshor. Now I don't have the means to go back home.
You should not be here. Your favors to the Wisp do not grant you favor with me.
Word spreads. You found Wyland. For all I know, its you that sent him to the spirit world to begin with.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: Do not dabble in the things you don't wish to dive into. This land is in trouble. The invaders here will be removed. We will restore our Goddess.
'Bout time Wickliff got a proper Inn and Pub. Won't be nothin' like that famed 'Goldshire' as far as fun times tends to go, but you can betcha it'll be somethin' special!
Adventure awaits out there. I just have to find a way off this island. I heard a rumor... nevermind. Don't wanna get anyone in trouble.
Just nevermind.
Been a while since we've had a ship come in. Wonder if I'ma be outta work soon...
You need somethin'?
You need somethin'?
You need somethin'?
This isn't how I expected life on Erenshor to go... I didn't intend to be a rebel. But here I am.
...is it really being a rebel though, if you had no other choice except to die? Port Azure put us all in a tough spot. My friends are there...
This is the last remaining Shrine to Sivakaya's form as it was before she fell. The Sivakayan warhorde has defiled the rest. This place must be protected.

Aimless brutes acting under a false banner. They do not serve Sivakaya, they use her power for destruction and fool themselves that it is in her honor. Why would she wish destruction on the world she helped create?

Just across the path they have defiled another Shrine. Silkengrass must have been an important place for Sivakaya, and so for that reason it is important to us. I'll make you a deal - if you help us protect this place, I will pay handsomely. Bring me Sivakayan Swords and Axes or even Sceptres as proof of your work.
Need something'? I've got spells and some artifacts for sale.
Need something'? I've got spells and some artifacts for sale.
... *it does not speak*
... *The Light flickers*

Hey there! Looking for any spells or skillbooks? I've got 'em! We're hoping to find more here too - I heard that The Wisp's soldiers are incredible with bows. Wouldn't know myself, but if they do and we can swap some knowledge with them, that'd be fantastic!

Things have been amiss in The Brake as of late. I've spotted Sivakayan Raiders passing through towards Port Azure. Why would they be going into the city? It's supposed to be the safest place on the continent...

The Governor has been consolidating its power in Port Azure. The soldiers aren't patrolling the continent any longer. There's just not enough manpower right now. Protecting the city is essential. The rest of us... we're on our own.
These Elixirs will make your adventures lucrative in ways you cannot imagine.
Try one, it's worth it.
Hello there traveler. What brings you to Willowwatch?

Our little corner of Erenshor. We're tucked away pretty safeley up here, and if you look over the clifftops to the East you can just make out Fernalla's Willow over in the Revival Plains. Lots of folks have been passin' through looking for shelter, but we cannot afford more to care for.
Treasures... found 'em all myself. Scraped 'em off things. Ripped 'em outta things.
We make our own way out here. We stay off the map. We're not a threat to the Sivakayans or anyone else. Restful Valley is about peace and harmony.
Goodsoil tried to establish itself as a town. Registered for trade with Port Azure and everything. Not us. We're just living off the land and minding our own here in Restful Valley.
Yarren? Yeah, he was just getting ready to move here from Goodsoil when the raid happened. I don't know where he ended up...

You will find the recipes for the flames among those who once followed Vitheo. But to kindle the flame of Life, you will need this gift from me for I have the only remaining one. Please, child... do what you must to put Vitheo to rest. Free him from Sivakaya's grasp. And may he find it in his heart to forgive me for the burden I placed upon him.

Child, you have my deepest gratitude for all you have done... yet there is still more to be faced. My body stands before you as a remnant of what it once was. Hollow and riddled with unnatural darkness and guilt. Vitheo cannot rest, nor can I.

Within the traitor's halls, you will find two braziers.  You must light them both, for only then will the abomination awaken. It holds the only key to Vitheo's sanctum... and I have the last remaining component for the life flame to ignite it. I bestow it upon you, please use it wisely.

Vitheo was spared the darkness that Sivakaya was able to wrap around me, but he still does not rest, held suspended by some necromancy, some attempt to pull him to her. She tries to reach his ascended being and take it for herself as she has done with me.

Vitheo has graduated this realm, more powerful than ever, as have I. But Sivakaya has claws in my ascended form. We must put Vitheo to rest before she gets his too, if she hasn't already. The key to his sanctum his held by a great abomination - placed there to protect Vitheo from those believed to be traitors - it is the only key. You must take it.

You will find the recipes for the flames among those who once followed Vitheo. But to kindle the flame of Life, you will need this gift from me for I have the only remaining one. Please, child... do what you must to put Vitheo to rest. Free him from Sivakaya's grasp. And may he find it in his heart to forgive me for the burden I placed upon him.

Child, you have my deepest gratitude for all you have done... yet there is still more to be faced. My body stands before you as a remnant of what it once was. Hollow and riddled with unnatural darkness and guilt. Vitheo cannot rest, nor can I.

Within the traitor's halls, you will find two braziers.  You must light them both, for only then will the abomination awaken. It holds the only key to Vitheo's sanctum... and I have the last remaining component for the life flame to ignite it. I bestow it upon you, please use it wisely.

Vitheo was spared the darkness that Sivakaya was able to wrap around me, but he still does not rest, held suspended by some necromancy, some attempt to pull him to her. She tries to reach his ascended being and take it for herself as she has done with me.

Vitheo has graduated this realm, more powerful than ever, as have I. But Sivakaya has claws in my ascended form. We must put Vitheo to rest before she gets his too, if she hasn't already. The key to his sanctum his held by a great abomination - placed there to protect Vitheo from those believed to be traitors - it is the only key. You must take it.
Faun, Druid of Fernalla at your service. Our kind has lived in this settlement since the days of the Great War.
There's more lost history here than will be written for centuries to come. This land was touched by Gods, and their blood spilled upon this very soil.
Talk to Malor. She's well versed.
Atop that plateau stands a Sivakayan place of power. It's been dormant for decades, but we keep watch just the same.
The Jaws of Sivakaya. Rumored to hold the decrepit form of her remains. It's sealed tight, with no apparent way to enter or exit.
It has never been accessed since it was sealed. Her followers do not even come here. The Sivakayans have made their home elsewhere.

Can see lot's of comings and goings from up here. Goliath keeps a good watch over this place. Keeps it quiet for the rest of us. Just the odd traffic into the cliffside over there to the north. Not even to the tunnel... just into the thickets... and do you see that smoke rising? I think something's in there. Don't dare explore it myself.
The sisterhood cares for this garden and its tree. The tree is said to have been planted by Fernalla herself as a reminder of the beauty of life!
Death is not a requirement of love and life, it is a choice made by our flawed Fernalla.
The Sisterhood of Fernalla is strong here. Praise Fernalla for her gift and for and all of her love!
Death is not a requirement of love and life, it is a choice made by our flawed Fernalla.
Fernalla's grace upon you, stranger. Her gift of life is strong!
Death is not a requirement of love and life, it is a choice made by our flawed Fernalla.
... *it flickers in and out of existence, as though its behind some unseen shroud*
Whatcha want? I'm dealing with a problem here.
If they're even real. If the jail's even real. Who knows. I'm trying to figure all that out now. You game to venture in? Keep what ya find, just bring me any intel that may be down there.

So they say, it caved in under weight of the castle itself. Buried men alive, including supposed prisoners. Guard Captain thinks there might be intel down there about the Sivakayans. Apparently the interrogation techniques were quite effective back then.

Yeah, been excavating this site here, lookin' to find that old castle basement that's rumored to be down there. Hit a snag... bugs. They're not that dangerous alone but they swarm! Can't get through 'em.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thanks again for finding that intel!

DialogText: Yes, a gift. If you share with me the bit of light that Kio has hidden in the tomb, then I will share a trinket with you. I must be sure that light never falls into Kio's hands again - neither this Kio nor the one you knew before.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Sleep well.

DialogText: Go back to reality, as it were. Not sure how long you're going to remember us. We'll remember you. Guess I can give you something to take along with you.
Just a figment of our imaginations. Funny though, how we all share it. How it creeps into our dreams with the same hurried steps. No matter, though. 'Spose you want to be finished with us then?
Well then, you've certainly done a number on this place, haven't you?
Everyone's afraid lately. The Sivakayans are being spotted all over the place, so I hear. Hard to get out and get anything I need lately.

I help cook for the guards. Keep 'em fed. That way they'll protect us I hope. On that note, I could use wolf meat and bear meat. As much of each as you can bring, I'll pay what I can. Can't get out to get my own lately.
I am freed from that body... the insanity it endured... that unwanted gift... it's all gone. Thank you.
When Astra came, Soluna fled. She left us here trapped by that beast. We could not sustain, and Astra's presence permeated the air, our minds were no match for it.
Astra has remained here, building power again, waiting to rise. I fear I may never truly rest until she is truly gone.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You are strong...

DialogText: I cannot remember who I was before. I was not a Paladin. Soluna twisted away from what I truly was and then she forged me into one of her children.
This is a great fishing spot. Hoping to get something soon! They sell poles up the road at the shop, if you're interested.
Haven't caught much. Got a few frownies, some old treasures, and a very used pair of boots.
A moongill? No. Haven't caught one of those. Just this old key. Not sure what it's to.
Got this old key. If you want it you can have it. I was just going to throw it back - doesn't look like any of the house keys 'round here.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Already gave it to ya!

DialogText: Port Azure left us. I hope Ripper takes out that whole city one day.
If it weren't for Ripper, we'd be undead servants to Sivakaya.
The Strand is not a place to linger. The blighted sand washes up more and more each day.
The currents bring the blight here, bit by bit. It attracts unpleasant things to the coast. It's been that way forever, I guess. Just didn't know it before I settled here.
A shame. It's a shame. Your budding presence is all wilted away.
The Sivakayans are trying to take back the land. The wardrums will descend on Port Azure soon enough if nothing is done. The Wisp alone cannot stop them.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: We're no concern of yours. Nor are you to us. You want friends? Some of your own are here too. Defectors, I believe.
They answer to someone called 'Ripper'. Go ask them, not me. They're clinging to those last few houses up the hill just to the west there.
It keeps things out. It keeps things in. Hard to tell which side we're on in this light.
RepeatingQuestDialog: It keeps things out. It keeps things in. Hard to tell which side we're on in this light.

DialogText: When those monsters came out... I thought it was the end. We shouldn't meddle in those things or else it'll be good bye blue sky for all of us.
Getting goods into this area is no trouble at all. There's smugglers running all the time.
I ain't going back in there. No way. Whole mine's filled with hellspawn.
Dug too far. Found somethin' we shouldn't of. Who knows what's crawled outta that pit?
Look hard enough, and you can find anything here. The Druids, the Arcanists... they've set up shop in town. The Paladins built the place, and we blades? We've got our own little corner too.
Look hard enough, and you can find anything here. The Druids, the Arcanists... they've set up shop in town. The Paladins built the place, and we blades? We've got our own little corner too.
Welcome to our little town. This place is a destination for all Paladins, and we aim to make a permanent home here for our people.
This is the very place where Soluna appeared on our plane. The power she left behind here can be sensed by anyone, but for a Paladin, it's... everything.
Welcome to our little town. This place is a destination for all Paladins, and we aim to make a permanent home here for our people.
This is the very place where Soluna appeared on our plane. The power she left behind here can be sensed by anyone, but for a Paladin, it's... everything.
Port Azure's hospitality has not been extended to us, though that's no surprise. We'll do just fine down here in the slums.
A reaver don't need much. A sword on his back, a battle in his future, and a mind to enjoy both. The slums don't play into those needs too much.
Well met traveller. Make yourself at home here. We have armors and weapons for sale among other rarities. May the wind of Vitheo guide thee ever forward.
Well met traveller. Make yourself at home here. We have armors and weapons for sale among other rarities. May the wind of Vitheo guide thee ever forward.
Well met traveller. Make yourself at home here. We have armors and weapons for sale among other rarities. May the wind of Vitheo guide thee ever forward.

Ah, not often I see a new face around here. Travel is sparse, only folks who stop in these days want a free bed for as long as I can give it. It's a dire state that a lot of folks are in these days. So much for a new life on Erenshor.

Aye, lost their homes... or too afraid to stay in 'em. Their dream of adventure and fortune has turned to a nightmare of uncertainty. I didn't come up with that - one of 'em said it the other day. Sad state, he was in.
We were on our way to bring some supplies to Goodsoil but... well, not anymore. I can't bring it back to Port Azure and I definitely can't get it to Ripper's Keep myself.
It's way up north and not an easy trek. Say... would you take this package for me? Bring me back the receipt? Leena should be more than obliged to accept it. It'd save her a trip down here.
RepeatingQuestDialog: I appreciate your help.

DialogText: Welcome, my Child. Take this place in, for its power outweighs any I've ever felt. Visit the shrine and statue while you're here. The guardians will not bother you.
Beings who rival only time in their age, they descended with Soluna and remained here as a sign of her presence.
Welcome, my Child. Take this place in, for its power outweighs any I've ever felt. Visit the shrine and statue while you're here. The guardians will not bother you.
Beings who rival only time in their age, they descended with Soluna and remained here as a sign of her presence.
Oh... you're warm, aren't you? That's nice. Unless you see it in a different light.
Oh - you're warm, aren't you? In this light, we can fix that. The scourge will come. He can make you cold... like us.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Oh... you're warm, aren't you? That's nice. Sometimes its hard to tell about folks in this light.

DialogText: Awful bunch. I used to have a shop, a home... there was a gorgeous waterfall right out my bedroom window. Port Azure pulled back the guards to defend the city and left us all alone. We had no choice but to leave.
What are you doing up here? You lose your life's work to the Sivakayans too?
I barely got away... Goodsoil, it's gone...
I don't know... I can't... I don't... why us? Where was Fernalla?
Whatcha doing 'round here? Stuck like the rest of us? Wickliff ain't such a bad little town. We're finally building an inn!
Get used to it. You're not finding your way off here any quicker than the rest of us. The rumors about the boatmen are a lie. The guards are all over it.
You think a boat small enough to not draw attention's going to be able to come and go as it pleases? The only boats that come here are the cargo ships that bring stowaways like you.
Howdy stranger. What brings you all this way up north? You a pilgrim?

Guess you're not. Well, we're here to protect what's left of the settlers up here. Kilkay and the goons in Port Azure pulled back the guard. We're not going to just sit and watch our own friends be slaughtered by Sivakayans.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You already gave him my message, didn't ya?

DialogText: Demons in human skin, those things. They're twisted by some sort of magic I've never seen. There's others around here too. They don't bug us, we don't bug them.
Keep to themselves mostly. Got a nice little thing going over in Loomingwood. If you've gotten this far, odds are they're ok with you being around. Just tread carefully.
The journey here was terrifying, but now... just look at this place, it's incredible.
That statue is the largest I've ever seen. Legend has it, it's a shell left behind by Soluna when she exited this place and returned home.
The journey here was terrifying, but now... just look at this place, it's incredible.
That statue is the largest I've ever seen. Legend has it, it's a shell left behind by Soluna when she exited this place and returned home.
You're not the average pilgrim. Beware the stardust floating about the crater. It's not what it seems. It feeds on the hope of unsuspecting explorers... it grants no wishes.
Many make the trek to see this place. It's one of the few in Erenshor where you can witness actual proof of the Gods.
I serve the vision of The Wisp.
'Ello there. Welcome to the Azure Arms.
Full house. With everyone moving back to the city, this place is in high demand.
Haven't seen you before, and i know ya didn't come off a boat. I learned not to ask questions though. No rooms right now, either way.
Up for some fishing? Got some supplies here!

Gotta get these beams up next. This place looks small to me, but the folks around here aren't that plentiful and travelers hardly ever walk up the hill. Should be fine, I s'pose. Would rather be fishing though.
My pal Garrey's always down in the port fishing. Catches all kinds of stuff, crazy stuff. He loves to talk about it, if ya ask him. Call 'em his treasures and won't refer to them any other way.
Welcome to Stowaway's Step. Please remain civil.
Welcome to Stowaway's Step. Please remain civil.
Welcome to Stowaway's Step. Please remain civil.
Where the willow stands, so shall stand a child of Fernalla. There are others from who we must protect the trees.
This willow stands in the eyeline of the Sivakayan statue atop the hill. Fernalla put this tree here as a sign of power. It's one of her most important willows.
Molorai. Wherever there are ruins, the Molorai are never far away. They're after something on the hilltop near the Sivakayan Ruins.
Watch out for Mephallo. Hides in the sky. Can't see him without help. I can't help.
Welcome to our little town of Wickliff. You get used to this place eventually except for that pink smoke... must be the ritual is underway again.
Most of us wanted to get to Erenshor... and for the right price there's still ways to get there.
I don't know anything more about it besides the whispers I've heard.
There's still boatmen who can cross the sea. They know the currents and winds. They don't work cheap though, and they don't work openly.
Nobody knows what the ritual is for. There are people on this island who are unaccounted for... they emerge from the forest and light the stones aflame - are they trying to summon something?
Made my way out here before all the trouble started. Not leavin' now. I'm not buggin' anyone, I expect nobody will bug me. Not the Sivayans or the other ones neither.
Sivakayans were just a whisper when I moved out here. Now, they're all over this place. And that cave up on the ledge there... don't even think about going in. You won't come out.

Call themselves "The Wisp" or something like that. Friendly enough, even stop in to check on me sometimes. They say they've got a place in that forest to the North, but I've never gone. They seem to have it out for the Sivakayans though, so they can't be all bad.
Got a few things left for sale until the next caravan comes through, if anything helps you, you're a welcome customer.

Deliveries are sparse now. Azure has closed the port, and that means what's here on Erenshor is all there is to be had. Not enough folks are willing to venture out to gather supplies, so we're pretty well rationed now.
Erenshor is our land, our birth right. The Paladins who run Port Azure can put us in the slums as we arrive, but we'll rise. They can't keep us from exposing their secret.
The Reavers are the first people of Amarion. We were here before anyone else. Erenshor is where we'll find the proof to our claim.

Yes. It's here. Our great ancestors are said to have lived on the north of an unknown continent, in a place where the barrier between reality and the void is worn thin. It's where we get our ancestral power.

I don't know where exactly. Many of us have come hoping to find a clue, or, with luck, may we even find our ancestral city still flourishing. If you find anything, please, bring it directly to me. I'll reward you for your efforts.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Any clues are welcome!

DialogText: They are not as different from us as they might hope. I suspect that the proof I want lies somewhere in the tale of Soluna's arrival, but cleverly omitted from their own origin story.
A pilgrim, eh? Not too common we see those.
You got Port Azure written all over you. You better be ready to be rid of it.
Abandoned their own folk up here in the North. Tried to pull us all back to town. Ripper wasn't havin' it. Now we're building our own place here. Kilkay don't like it.
Ripper marched right up to the door and we kicked the Sivakayans out. Place was crawlin' with 'em. Not anymore. They're not that tough.
Somethin' is off about that lot. The only writings we found didn't even mention Sivakaya. They're all bent about someone named Azynthi.
Who knows. Found an odd magical trinket. Maybe it was his. Tell ya what, you want it? Bring me 3 bottles of Azure Mead and some Seaspice. I'll trade ya. I miss Azure Mead.
I've heard whispers of a hidden crack in the rocks to the east that leads to a hidden valley... the source was a drunkard, and as unreliable as any I've overheard before.
This place has attracted many types over the years. Some more ambitious in others in their quest for fortune.
I... I can't remember what happened. I just remember the phrase they kept saying...
It was "Sivak Sivak", what do you think that means? Should we tell someone? I haven't told anyone...
Yarren's missing? He's not here?? Oh no... he was a bit of an... apologist... he had some strange views on the Sivakayans. I hope he didn't do something rash.
Sivakaya can be restored. Her armies are not acting on her will, I am convinced of that.
They're power hungry, lead by someone else. Sivakaya has not been seen or heard from in ages. Something is harnessing her energy.
I'm at a loss. My husband is missing. Our home is in shambles, I spend all my time working just to survive.
He was searching for a lost treasure. He would stop back here between his expeditions but I have not seen him in months now. I fear the worst.
You one of the slummers?

Down behind the market square, where the poor folk live. Came to Erenshor without a coin to spare, now they live in the pits of Azure. I hear all sorts of unsavories come out at night over there. Wouldn't know, I stay away.
I have seen her. I've had her image seared into my mind.
Sivakaya came to me in a vision. An empty shell of a Goddess and filled with decay. I fear we may have to fell her current form to restore her former one.
It would take a dozen warriors or more. She was reeking of dark magic. We must find a way to summon her, we must continue the work.
Just bought this place. Saved up for years. Never sold a thing til there was a deal! Climbed all over the world looking for deals, you see! Spent whole nights fishing and mining! Now I'm rich!
Never met a mountain or statue I couldn't climb. Been all over this island. Fell into caves. Fell into the ocean. Fell off cliffs bigger than you can imagine. It was all worth it!
Port Azure left us on our own. I lived in a beautiful place called the Silkengrass Meadow. Then the Sivakayans came.
Never thought I'd be living in an inn. I came to Erenshor for a new start, not for this.
When you stare at the sea too long, it can start to peer back... it's full of eyes. So many eyes... You have eyes! And your eyes are quite nice! Perhaps I may borrow them?
I wish it was that simple...
He will like them! Please, I only need one...
Do not look at him! Do not let him see your eyes!
Oh, fancy seeing you here.
Here! There! And where else? Like the book thrown to the sea, you surface!
You're not going to believe them!
Oh, fancy seeing you here.
Here! There! And where else?
You're not going to believe them!
A light to carry! You'll scare the frights! You'll need it to see, in this light!
RepeatingQuestDialog: Yes! A light to see!

DialogText: When you stare at the sea too long, it can start to peer back... it's full of eyes. So many eyes... You have eyes! And your eyes are quite nice! Perhaps I may borrow them?
I wish it was that simple...
He will like them! Please, I only need one...
Do not look at him! Do not let him see your eyes!
This place... I come here to feel the power of it.
It's one of the rare places two Gods have touched. Fernalla's willow, and Sivakaya's temple standing guard against one another... like a timeless duel.
It's all so vulgar. I don't care if they're suspected Sivakayan agents or Molorai or anyone else. Hanging is wrong. We need to go back to burning them like civilized humans.
It's you... the one who found Wyland and freed him. We are indebted.
I am something of an expert on the history of this land and on Sivakaya. I don't know in what form that might be helpful, but I am here.

The Candle belonged to The First Wisp - Eldrin. It was stolen from us long ago by a spy in the night. I would suspect it has found its way into the hands of Sivakayans... possibly the Darkwardens. Maybe its buried in the sands of Braxonia, or, worse yet, in some antiquities shop somewhere. If you find any trace of it, bring it to me.

Yes, I have taken great interest in the old Sivakayan Churches we've found. I suspect that the great skull cave in Duskenlight may be one of them, because its entrance resembles images of the Nighthollow Candle.
Do not disturb things you do not understand.
He must never leave...

I run supplies for Ripper. The trip to Port Azure is long, but I can get there in a couple of days. My associate in Port Azure also makes the odd trip up here too. We have to keep it all quiet, though.

Careful, good old Rattlewrists is out again. That pesky skeleton... we just can't be rid of him. You can throw him in the sea and he just shows up again later on that same day. Don't be afraid to smack him around, he's pretty harmless.
He's a local celebrity at this point. He tips over pretty easy, and you look strong. Just bop him one.

Careful, good old Rattlewrists is out again. That pesky skeleton... we just can't be rid of him. You can throw him in the sea and he just shows up again later on that same day. Don't be afraid to smack him around, he's pretty harmless.
He's a local celebrity at this point. He tips over pretty easy, and you look strong. Just bop him one.
You have no business here tonight.
By the will of the wisp, we shall know only greatness.
Another Paladin is always welcome here. Take comfort in this place, it was the first building raised in Port Azure. Praise Soluna! Have you found your destiny yet?
Your destiny is for you alone to determine. For some, it's power and for others it's fortune. For me, it's Soluna's Celestial Blade.
The very sword Soluna bestowed upon her avatar here in these lands and now lost to time, it's nothing short of fabled. Where to look - that's another question that I do not have the answer to.
Another Paladin is always welcome here. Take comfort in this place, it was the first building raised in Port Azure. Praise Soluna! Have you found your destiny yet?
Your destiny is for you alone to determine. For some, it's power and for others it's fortune. For me, it's Soluna's Celestial Blade.
The very sword Soluna bestowed upon her avatar here in these lands and now lost to time, it's nothing short of fabled. Where to look - that's another question that I do not have the answer to.
Leave me alone. I came here to make my own way, and I don't need trouble that all you folks drag along in your wake.
Just go. You have nothing I want.
The walk here isn't bad. Just up the Duskenlight Coast and watch out for the wildlife. The fishing is worth it.
We're going to find a way back to Stowaway's Step. Want to help? From there, we think we can find our way back home.

There's a fishing boat that sometimes stops on the beach in Blacksalt Strand. We've both seen it. If we can make a ring that enchants our fishing skill, we can use it to buy a ride back. Here's a template, if you can find the parts...
RepeatingQuestDialog: Any luck? If you've made the ring already, show me!

DialogText: There's nothing left here except ruins... we've lost an entire village.
The druids say it was Sivakayan raiders. We can't be sure. There is a small camp of survivors just through the field, if you wish to speak to them...
The Sivakyans are being spotted all over... these little towns used to have the support of Port Azure - but we've had to pull back now to protect our larger interests.
I don't know these folks. Ask over at the camp.

The wards we make hold back the Sivakayan hoardes so that we might work. The Loomingwood is well protected... but the wards tend to attract things that can be more dangerous than a single Sivakayan. Stay within the wardlight if you go there.
We perform these rituals to draw power from the land, searching for places where Sivakaya may have tread... where we might summon her home and restore her.
I cannot recall my purpose nor how I came to be here, but I am drawn to this place. I think I shall stay.
You're back... I've heard that you've been busy.
Yes, we have followed your exploits. We believe you'll be a great help to us. Have you found anything of interest? If you need to go back to Erenshor, I can assist with that too.
Haven't you said that word to me before? Did you find something else?
The most obscure of items, I would doubt you would find it on this plane. Perhaps the answer lies within a dream...
A gem forged in sadness - I would look for this in places that Brax and Sivakaya may have once shared.
A piece of her. She was shattered on Erenshor, there must be remnants. Likely amongst her followers.

You will know its host when you see it. It'll be twisted and aggressive - it will not be one of her followers, we have learned that with certainty. While they show symptoms, the dementia does not seem to take hold of them.
Here, take this. It'll save you the boat ride if you don't have one yet. Otherwise just toss it away.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Ah nevermind, I was going to give you a Rune of Hidden Hills but you have it already.

DialogText: Begone. *Lucian's eyes never leave the searing flames before him*.
...Hawthorne? Who are you? Where did you learn that word? If you've found something, then give it to me now and we'll talk about... about what you know.
Have you seen him?

Hey there. Welcome to our little corner of Erenshor. I've got some skill books and some spells for sale, if you're still learning. If you want a task to do, I've got one of those I could use a hand with too.

If you have time, head north into that cave in the cliffside. They call it The Bonepits. There are these awful things called Avatars of Sivakaya deep in there. They're good for one thing - their horns make great sharpening stones... but they break easy. Bring me one of their horns intact, and I'll make it worth your while.
AssignQuestStableKey: quest:a sivakayan horn for ludo luxo

DialogText: We will remain on this land, it is our sacred right.
Fernalla ascended from Erenshor, and we, her children, shall serve her from this place.
When a hero perishes, they might ascend to become a God or Goddess, as Fernalla did in the war against Sivakaya. Her willows will stand here eternally, and so shall we.

They spawned from Fernalla herself, as she rose to her Plane of the Great Willow. She sowed the seeds herself, gifting us her eternal presence. The very gate through which she stepped is still standing!
It's in the Revival Plains, quite a sight to see! Legend has it that the one who approaches the gate with a worthy gift will gain entry!
We cannot say, something otherworldly - perhaps something from her brother or her father - both of whom I'm sure she misses dearly. We have tried every wordly thing, to no avail.
By mercy of the wisp, you may leave.
By the will of the wisp, we shall know only greatness.
It's stunning how large it is... no human could have made this. It makes true believers out of any who see it.
You know how to swim? I'm thinking of learning. It looks so inviting...
You know how to swim? I'm thinking of learning. It looks so inviting...
You know how to swim? I'm thinking of learning. It looks so inviting...
I... I'm in shock. I feel so numb. I wish you were here to help earlier.
Well then, wasn't expecting one of you to show up. Kilkay send ya? What's the message?

Biggest coward I ever heard of. No message? Well, I sure got one for him. Would like to deliver it on the end o' my axe, but this'll do for now. Give him this if you see 'im. Tell him it's from Ripper's guys.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You already gave him my message, didn't ya?

DialogText: Pulling all his soldiers out of the Northlands, abandoning these pilgrim towns to fend for themselves, and then watching the smoke from their ruins float over the walls from the safety of Port Azure. If he isn't going to go to war with the Sivakayans, we will. And then he'll be next.

We've been watching their movements for the last few months. They're coming through the pass, but we haven't pushed much beyond that yet. There's another group up here, calls themselves 'The Wisp'. They seem harmless, and let us pass without much trouble.

Seem to be bent on defeating the Sivakayans too, I've seen 'em not hesitate to fight. I get the idea that there's more to their story though. Don't got time to ask, or care. As long as their blades aren't pointed at us, they're ok in my book.

We're the last shred of humanity on this continent I'd say. Rest of 'em left their own to die out here to the Sivakayans. Ripper'd seen enough, and took our squad north to try and help the last Pilgrims. Our guys have a keep on the other side of the Loomingwood. We could probably use someone like you.
We're waiting for orders to blow the mine and seal it off.
If I see them come through there, I'm running! They're so scary!

Can't wait til that mining camp is run outta here once and for all. Be it bandits, or some other monstrosity they dig up - I don't care. I just want 'em gone. All the wildlife 'round here is all stirred up 'cause of them.
Those red tents just down the way. Don't know who sent 'em or why they're here but they all of a sudden took a lot of interest in these cliffs.
I am Myrre, I am an emissary of Eldoth Molorai. Do you have business?
He is the Savior of Erenshor. He will prevent Port Azure from destroying this land. He knows things that you do not.
Port Azure is a disease upon this land, spreading like a fungus and destroying the divinity that has been preserved here.
Erenshor is a land of the Gods. You and your kind have no right to any of it. Eldoth will protect it.

Eldoth is no longer welcome in Port Azure, they have exiled him in their greed and blindness. I am here on his behalf, listening for whispers of artifact exchanges so that we might intervene. You may not conduct trades without the approval of Eldoth.
Welcome to Wardhaven. These woods are safe from Sivakayans. Here is where we can craft our plans in peace. Bless the ward!
You may have noted the gates along the path. Sivakayans cannot cross them - their unnatural dark magic is torn apart by the natural energy of those gates.
Praise Soluna! We are so fortunate to be her followers. The proof of her having been here is undeniable!
Well there, stranger. You're a long way from home. Your kind doesn't fare well away from the city.
There's Sivakayans about. Among other things.
We must continue the work.
We harvest energy from the land, searching for traces of Sivakaya within it. When we find the place, we'll be able to summon her there, from wherever it is she's bonded.
It would take a dozen warriors or more. She was reeking of dark magic. We must find a way to summon her, we must continue the work.
Used to be quite the adventurer... now I'm washed up. Old injuries, ya know. Couldn't even protect my grandfather's lucky coin from those bandits.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Can't believe you found it!

DialogText: Don't know where they hide out. It's a small island... can't be that hard to find, if you've got the gumption.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You really cleaned out that  cave, didn't ya!

DialogText: Bandits got it. They're somewhere on the island... Told me I could have it back for 300 gold. I don't have 300 gold and I don't have the oomph to fight 'em anymore either.
