RepeatingQuestDialog: You really showed those bandits!

DialogText: If you're learning the ways of the wizard, I have some spells you may need. Buy 'em or don't, doesn't bother me any. Need a job to do? Got one of them too.
If you head over into Faerie's Brake, and you can track down one of those nasty little Faeries, kill it. If it has any Faerie dust, bring it to me. I've got something for you if you do.
If you're learning the ways of the wizard, I have some spells you may need. Buy 'em or don't, doesn't bother me any. Need a job to do? Got one of them too.
If you head over into Faerie's Brake, and you can track down one of those nasty little Faeries, kill it. If it has any Faerie dust, bring it to me. I've got something for you if you do.
Feed me...
Yesssss....
Feed me...
Yesssss....
Feed me...
Yesssss....
... *It does not respond*
Ay there, 'fraid I can't offer ya much. Molorai folks cleaned me out.

Came through in droves from the pass a few days back. Half went up that hill, other half kept on down the road, not sure where to. Didn't pay the worth of what they took, neither. Not much I can do 'bout that though, it's just me ya know.
I stopped here for a rest before continuing on my pilgrimage. The journey along the coast was breathtaking... but tiresome.
This is the best I can do for myself for the time being. It's not much but it feels safe. I have some spells I acquired in a trade. If you want to buy them, it would help me more than you know.

Happened across a trader who was in need of food. Had bags and bags full of spells and told me I'd get more value for these once I found a buyer. That'll get me closer to finding a true home, so I figured it was worth the risk.
Fellow child, Fernalla bless you. Step into our circle and stay a while. Our cause and crusade continues.
Fernalla willing, we'll recover the fallen branch of Fernalla's willow, and our chosen one will wield it in our name.

A tree so large it can't be seen by human eyes. A tree so lush it cannot be felt by human hands. Its branches fall from the heavens and land with such impact that a human does not hear. The one who holds the branches, sees all and becomes everything.
Fellow child, Fernalla bless you. Step into our circle and stay a while. Our cause and crusade continues.
Fernalla willing, we'll recover the fallen branch of Fernalla's willow, and our chosen one will wield it in our name.

A tree so large it can't be seen by human eyes. A tree so lush it cannot be felt by human hands. Its branches fall from the heavens and land with such impact that a human does not hear. The one who holds the branches, sees all and becomes everything.
Fellow child, Fernalla bless you. Step into our circle and stay a while. Our cause and crusade continues.
Fernalla willing, we'll recover the fallen branch of Fernalla's willow, and our chosen one will wield it in our name.

A tree so large it can't be seen by human eyes. A tree so lush it cannot be felt by human hands. Its branches fall from the heavens and land with such impact that a human does not hear. The one who holds the branches, sees all and becomes everything.
We've worked hard to establish this place. I think Ripper's looking to go to war with Port Azure, I just hope that war doesn't get dragged back here.
Not sure what we'd have done without Ripper. I'm just waiting to be conscripted into service, but it hasn't happened yet.
... *It blinks sadly. A small key materializes in your hand*
... *It stares intently*
Rings and Jewels! Finest in the land.
Careful around old ruins! They tend to attract creatures. Worse yet, sometimes they contain things that have never left.

Just up over that hill to the south for example - those ruins are overrun by some sort of ancient guardians that have been there for as long as time remembers. Not sure about their origin, but they don't like visitors.
Whaddaya want?
RepeatingQuestDialog: You're not that gullible, are ya?

DialogText: I know a guy who can get you to Erenshor. First, bring me some Sea Spice. Love the stuff. Hooked on it even. I'll get you whatever you need after that.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You're not that gullible, are ya?

DialogText: You wanna go to Erenshor? I'm your guy. You gotta do me a favor first though. I need to know you're legit.
Fernalla's blessings to you. Do you bring new news?
Pray, if you bring news then share it generously, lest we remain lost.
Pierson Windwash at your service. I've collected quite a variety of goods here, if you're in the market.
Croc season. We need their tails. Got any?
RepeatingQuestDialog: Still need more croc tails!

DialogText: Cros are in season. We're here to hunt.
We have given all of ourselves...
We hold him imprisoned... forever...
Has he asked you to free him from us? You musn't. He is not what he says...

A demon... our own summoning of which we've lost control... we lured it here long ago, before humans settled the island. We restrain his true form. If you destroy us, our watchlights... he will be freed, and you will be his. Leave us be... or dispatch of him yourself.

Ask Stalzo for the Empty Soul Stone. If you hand it to Aragath, it will absorb his life power and weaken him enough for you to destroy him. He will no longer be a danger to this world and we will be able to leave this place forever.
Welcome! This is my bank wagon. I can store anything you need. Even give it to your pals, by your request of course.
Welcome! This is my bank wagon. I can store anything you need. Even give it to your pals, by your request of course.
Siva-whatevers. I have piles of 'em. I trade one for a map and elixir. Don't negotiate neither. Bring 'em if you got extras.
Those funny little potions you get over from the oddities folks. I'm on their second-to-least strongest one. Anything more is just too much for this head of mine.
You wanderin' around lost? E'ryone knows you bring me maps and Elixirs, I give you one of those junk coins I got out of a chest.
Ho there! You bring a map? An Elixir?
Tough livin' out here these days. Thankfully, Ripper's men stayed behind. Port Azure is a city of cowards!
He's a hero. Took an entire keep from the Sivakayans, made a home there. He keeps men out this way too. Port Azure lost a lot of its forces to him when they wrote us off for dead.
Step away from the Reliquary gate, citizen. It's a place that best remains undisturbed.

It's a strange place. It's not in any of the Port Azure construction plans. There's no record of who built it during construction, but here it is. Full of odd things. It could use some repair, but nobody is willing to enter.

There's something off about it. The Governer of Port Azure wants it off his mind. Folks say it's haunted, even. I'd believe anything now-a-days. Tell you what... you take a peek in there. If you like what you see, you be its caretaker. I don't care what you do in there, just leave me out of it.
The portal can be re-activated with a piece of Vitheo's tomb to link it... perhaps a stone or brick from its walls?
RepeatingQuestDialog: Siblings are linked in many ways...

DialogText: We are close to something here.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go away!

DialogText: You're not part of this.
What did you just say? You need to talk to Lucian... right now.
Have you found the... hey! You're not Wyland! Don't disturb us.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go away!

DialogText: What did you just say? You need to talk to Lucian... right now.
You're not part of this.
Why have you come here?
Aragath... *hissss*
I found a bit of Elderstone Ore. It shines like some crazy diamond... I bet there's more down there.
A life of solitude out here. It's all we want. Port Azure stirs up too much trouble. They know we've split off, but we don't care. Restful Valley is our own place.
Yarren? Yeah, he says he's moving down here to the valley.
You'd best have a purpose for addressing me. Did Kilkay send you?
We don't take in folks we don't know.

Kilkay's got a standin' order for my head so it only seems fair I place one for his. Can't find anyone brave enough to go and get it for me though. Nobody's willin' to give up the luxuries of Port Azure.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You did a good job, and we all appreciate it.

DialogText: We've got everything they do 'cept the looks. Far as we're concerned, the Azure Guard is the enemy. The folks who live there? They won't jump into a tussle, most of 'em. Heck, most of 'em don't even *like* the guard. Betcha the guard would turn tail and run if the Sivakayans showed up, too.

Softies. We took this place from them without even a scratch on our guys. Know what's funny? They've got no allegience to Sivakaya that I can find. All their writings are about 'Azynthi', whoeever that is. They're still lurking around though. If you find anything, please bring it my way.
RepeatingQuestDialog: They're always going to be trouble for us.

DialogText: Mystery figure far as I know. Heard some legends about him corrupting Sivakaya through trickery long ago, locking her away and stealing her power. The folks over in Loomingwood seem to be on that track too. Ask them, not me,
For Ripper.
Not my ideal day, standing around up here... staring at those things.
We have to rebuild that wall... I can't get those echoes out of my mind... I have to get home to mother!
Old Rook knows the truth! All these rocks they dig up got secrets in 'em, bet your britches on it. Not sellin 'em for less than a million gold each. Take it or leave it.
Got some instructional books for sale here.
They're to help ya learn. Nothin' special.
Got books for sale.
Yep, I sell books.  If you find any rare ones, bring 'em my way and show 'em to me before you hold out your hand askin' for money.
I am Rose, emmissary for Eldoth Molorai. You may conduct any official business through me. Do not venture beyond this point, by order of Eldoth.
Eldoth is working to protect the history of this land so that he might restore it in a way for all to enjoy.

The Molorai are investigating a potential place of interest atop this ridge. You will be met with force, as our soldiers have been educated, and they understand that any outsider is a threat to the Molorai mission.
The Gods will return to Erenshor through our leader. None will stand in the way of that. It is for the best.
Immeasurably beautiful, and guarded by two demons - one made of stone, and one made of fear.
I see a beast, ancient and foggy, mouth full of fangs. It's wet, and I'm tired from swimming.
About great bridges across the sky. About underwater caves, full of walking dreams.
I'm tired. I'm having nightmares.
Those men on the hill... they're trying to get into the jaws.
The Jaws of Sivakaya... that ancient temple with the skullstone entrance. Nobody's been able to open it for a lifetime. They think they're going to figure it out... Who knows what that'll unleash!
I'm talkin' here. Not to you, neither.
Ah, welcome to our home. We don't get many strangers around here. Some of these folks may not take too kindly to ya. Don't take it personal. I've got some things for sale, if you need anything.
Our little settlement, after everyone fled back to Port Azure there was no room for us. We're far enough from trouble out here and many of us are capable in a fight.
Port Azure? Just a small walk to the east. If you're headed that way, can you take this note to my good friend there?
His name is Harvey Alder. Probably staying at the Azure Arms for lack of any other place to go.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thank you for delivering my note.

DialogText: We don't get many strangers around here. Some of these folks may not take too kindly to ya. Don't take it personal. I've got some things for sale, if you need anything.
Hey there! Not too often a stranger stops by my little corner of the world! I've got some stuff for sale if you wanna buy anything.
Well met. You buyin'? Typically I just get business from the Militia.
Yup. They're not the most pleasant bunch, probably won't welcome you if you pop around. I keep 'em fed though. Business is business.
Well met. You buyin'? Typically I just get business from the Militia.
Yup. They're not the most pleasant bunch, probably won't welcome you if you pop around. I keep 'em fed though. Business is business.
Well met. You buyin'? Typically I just get business from the Militia.

They're not the most pleasant bunch, probably won't welcome you if you pop around. I keep 'em fed and they clear out the Sea Giant Ghosts every now and again and keep me stocked in aetheria. Business is business, you know.
Any ghost can leave it behind once you dispatch him, but It's rare. I pay well for it and for an adventurer like you, I've got something a little more unique than gold  if you find any.
Another Paladin is always welcome here. Take comfort in this place, it was the first building raised in Port Azure. Praise Soluna! Have you found your destiny yet?
Your destiny is for your alone to determine. For some, it's power and for others it's fortune. For me, it's Soluna's Flameblade.
The very sword Soluna bestowed upon her avatar here. Lost in the battle with Sivakaya, it's nothing short of fabled. Where to look - that's another question that I do not have the answer to.
This house just sold. You just missed the deal of a lifetime, let me tell you! Guy moving in is a bit of a character. He had a lot of stuff.
It was 1.25 million gold. A fair price, for the size!
Someone beat ya to it. Sorry!
Sorry to hear that you feel that way.
Careful down in the forest. It's a rough place... ugh... being a guard is awful. I want to be something more... maybe a emissary... or, no... an ambassador!
I... I left my shovel. It's a family heirloom. One of those slime-things slithered over it and it was gone!
If you find it, can I have it back? I know it's not important, but... but it meant a lot to me.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thank you for returning it.

DialogText: It astounds me how quickly Port Azure abandoned this place.
Turned tail and ran home at the first whisper of Sivakayans. They don't even know the truth.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: There is more to Sivakaya than you know. Her power grows and her corruption with it. If she is not stopped, more than just Erenshor will fall to her hand.
Kiddo, what have you done to this place? Your hands look empty but that ship out there is not right! Let's go back to Azure, I told ya I'm not one for ghost stories!
Ahoy there! Do me a favor, 'eh kiddo? My back's not what it used to be - could you hand me that sack on the ground there?
I need Octorunners, that's it. I need 4 of 'em to finish my quota and then I can sail you out there. Bring 'em on over and let's go.

They say he left it all behind in some sad effort to repent. If ya ask me or anyone else kiddo, he's got a load of it on that island. Promise to cut me in, eh? I didn't explore none of it, kiddo, I swear. Left it just as I found it. We going or what? Just gotta square away my daily catch first.

You don't know the tale? Ah, well, I s'pose if you're not fishin' you don't got time to tell stories. Bellwain is the most wanted Pirate in history. Terrorized Merosavilla for decades, then fled away when he got swept up by some barmaid he met. Loved to leave notes, claimed he changed his ways and just wanted peace. Even left his treasure behind for the takin'. So they say.  I'll take ya out there even, just gotta help me with today's catch is all, I got quotas ya know.
I ain't much for ghost stories kiddo I'm fixin to stay here and fish. If you find anything valuable just promise to cut me in. When you're ready to go back, just hop on my boat. I'll get the hint.
Wouldn't miss a chance to take what this land offers. If it means livin' in the slums of the city, then that's what it means.
Port Azure don't owe us nothin' and we're not givin nothin' back to her either. Seems fair to me.

It's beginning to manifest itself! You must take from it that which it took from me. It possesses the secrets of this world. Bring them to The Wisp. For now, I must go. We will meet again, but I fear it will not truly be... me... when we do.
You must end this corruption. It has touched us all. Fernalla. Vitheo. Brax. Even Soluna bears its mark. We endure in fractured shells. Free us.
I hid this last remnant of myself here. I called to my followers, but few still listen. At last, you have answered.
Azynthi has taken my very being from me. I am but a shard that remains, and I cling to the light. He is ceaseless. He is hunting for power, and he is finding it.

You have come. Quickly - the darkness clings to you and all who walk this world. It has followed you and will manifest here soon. When it does, I cannot remain. I am not whole, and it will take what little I have left.
Goodbye.
Had a dream about a cave here... a bear made of dead dreams lived in it, and I was soaked... freezing.
Such a vivid dream, so I came to see. But there's nothing here. Funny though, I remember being so wet...
Don't fret. Fishing always clears the mind.
Welcome, Child of Soluna.
Welcome, Child of Soluna.
Welcome, Child of Soluna.

A place of rebirth invites reinvention. Quiet your confusion, set right your wrongs, and stand as yourself.

(At this place, you may respec your proficiencies, and those of your friends - open your inventory or theirs)
Fernalla took her true form in this very spot, and left behind The Willow in her wake. Her gift to all of us is the opportunity to find our own truths.
Like everywhere else in Erenshor, here there are no houses to be had for the displaced here in Willowwatch. Are you one of the unfortunate? Best of luck to ya if you are.
So many little towns and villages have fled to Port Azure for safety. We had our own little garrison of guards here too, we gave them quarter, but now they have departed on orders back to the city.
Port Azure has preserved the willow there, thanks to the Fernallan Sisters. We Druids of Fernalla are not as welcome within its walls.

Useful imposters, they are. They claim to follow Fernalla but, just like the rest of their kind, they dismiss the parts of her nature that they do not enjoy. We Druids embrace all parts of nature. Even the unpleasant.
It grows right in the Port Azure square. The city was built around it. It's good to respect the Gods. Fernalla's blessings have fallen upon the city for their piety.
What're you doing way out here? Lost like the rest of us? Headed to the ruins?

In the right light you can see the beaches of Erenshor off in the distance. Too foggy today, but it's out there. I've seen a ship come back and forth in the night - unmarked, too. No idea who's sailing it, but there's something going on...
Just down the path there. The old fortress. Wouldn't go down there at night if I were you. Odd place, that.
It's been centuries since we've been graced with a living presence...
Aragath...
You will be pleased with how effective this will be...
RepeatingQuestDialog: I've given it to you already...

DialogText: We are lost. We are doomed. Azynthi will not help us.
We are lost. We are doomed. Azynthi will not help us.
We are lost. We are doomed. Azynthi will not help us.
We are lost. We are doomed. Azynthi will not help us.
If you do not belong here, do not pass by me. The warmth of your greeting will be provided by your own escaping blood. Prove yourself. State the passphrase.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You're welcome here.

DialogText: Say it or leave.
Wrong group.
Well, someone must have taken a liking to you. We're about to uncover something incredible here, and we need help. Go in and speak to the others.
... *it does not speak*

You can't have anything. It's for the guards. Unless you do me a favor. Then we can talk.

I need Sivakayan ore. Bring me 4 chunks and I'll get you something worth your while for it. It's hard to come by. You'll find a bit of it on their soldiers from time to time. I'm trying to figure out how to replicate their weapons... those things never go dull.
RepeatingQuestDialog: I have all I need for now, thank you.

DialogText: The Windblades of Erenshor are all welcome in this place. We work together towards common goals.
We hunt for lost treasures. Armors. Weapons. You name it. We follow clues and we find them. The Vithean Winds guide us.

Ripper trusts you. I look out for her, make myself known when needed. I stay in the shadows when I'm not. The shadows would be convenient for you too now. Depriving oneself of the luxuries of Port Azure isn't easy.

I have something for you. I need a favor in return though. I need the rare intact fungal cap from Abyssal Lake. It's crawling with Sivakayans. Beyond them, there is a lake crawling with living fungus. Every now and again, one of them will leave behind an intact cap. Bring me 3 of them.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Those fungal caps will be very useful!

DialogText: We're building a new home here. Ripper will protect us. Port Azure isn't safe... there's something amiss there.
Nothing to do these days but drink and wait for the Sivakayans to decend upon us from wherever they're lurking.
Incredible place, isn't it? You've been here before.
Yes! Inside the Dreams!
Oh, pity you. You don't know with what you meddle. No worry, toddle off and play some more. I have a surprise for you to wake us up! Bring me proof of your victory.
Oh well... the game is over. Inconsequential, isn't it? Your prize - but do not tell Kio! He likes the island in this light!
RepeatingQuestDialog: Eeheheh!!

DialogText: Oi, I'm the broker here in Port Azure. Got buyin' or sellin' to do? I'm your best way to do it.
Oi, I'm the broker here in Port Azure. Got buyin' or sellin' to do? I'm your best way to do it.
Got a room here. It's nice enough, maybe one day I'll work up enough savings to get a house here in the city. Not like you can just build your own here...
Those Sivakayans must have marched right by my door. I didn't hear a thing... I can't stay here anymore after this - what if they came here??
Those Sivakayans must have marched right by my door. I didn't hear a thing... I can't stay here anymore after this - what if they came here??
Stowaway's Step is home now. Like it or not. Erenshor's out of reach for most of us. I just wish that ritual wouldn't happen so often.
You ain't gettin' there without knowin' someone who can take ya.
We just don't know. They won't talk to us. The guards have confronted them, but they do not speak. Saw 'em headed back and forth from the old tomb once or twice... not sure why.
Down the stairs into the forest, at the end of the path you can get into the old island tomb. Not sure who's buried down there, really. Creepy place though.
RepeatingQuestDialog: You been there yet?

DialogText: Fernalla's grace to you, traveller. I have some things that may aide you in our cause. Please, buy whatever would benefit you.
Fernalla's grace to you, traveller. I have some things that may aide you in our cause. Please, buy whatever would benefit you.
Fernalla's grace to you, traveller. I have some things that may aide you in our cause. Please, buy whatever would benefit you.
I think we dug into something we shouldn't have down there - I saw demons!
We hit a chamber... they came swarming towards the opening and they were clawing at it to get through so we ran. We ran all the way out here, but if they come out here too what are we going to do?
Not many of us left here. The excursions have taken their toll.

We venture out to complete our mission. Some don't return or are held away. Sivakaya's power has been shattered across the land. It is imperative that we restore her... though, it seems each day that she's more lost than before. Could use some help here locally, if you're looking for work?
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: A few rarities to collect in the area that we use for our rituals. I need a pink Pteriaped feather, a Devoratrix thorn, and - this is a tough one - an intact faerie wing.
RepeatingQuestDialog: I'm all set for now, thank you.

DialogText: Oh, hi there. I'm just here to announce the latest news. Right now I'm recognizing the folks who have contributed to help the development of our fine island!
Oh, hi there. I'm just here to announce the latest news. Right now I'm recognizing the folks who have contributed to help the development of our fine island!
This place isn't so bad. None of us expected to live here when we arrived... but when they stopped all immigration to Erenshor we really had no choice.
Sivakayans? They're just the folks who worship the Goddess of War - Sivakaya. Battle is in their souls and their targets are anyone outside of their own circle.
There's a war coming to Erenshor. The Sivakayans aren't afraid anymore, and they're causing trouble all over.

Oi, welcome to the Island traveller. If you need supplies, I have them. If you're passin' through, well, you may be here a while still. Remember that I'm here and do yourself a favor and avoid the ritual. Looks like they started it up again.
There's no tellin' what it's for. We stopped askin' and they do their thing and leave us alone. Just bugs me... we don't know where they even come from. Must be some hidden place on the island.
There's a little settlement up ahead. Stop in and see if they can help ya. If you want real gear though, head through the Brake and into the City. You won't find much useful way out here.

There's a little settlement up ahead. Stop in and see if they can help ya. If you want real gear though, head through the Brake and into the City. If you're looking for jobs to do, I've got one for you. Need it done quick before we move out, though.

We're lookin' into Molorai. Not just for our missing person, but we suspect he's got something to do with the rise in Necromancy here on Erenshor. I heard rumors there might even be a Necromancer or two in the bonepits. You wanna go and ask one real nice for his ritual book, I'd love to have a look at it.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Got my Necro Ritual Book yet?

DialogText: It's a little unsuspecting cave just to the North in this area. See that big statue on the clifftop? You can see it from here. Bonepits sits right under that.
You know anything bout swords? I've come into possession of a few that are real special. I might pass one of 'em over to you, for a favor...

Color me surprised.  I figured you wouldn't have the time to bother.  I need some Seaspice - love the stuff, hate huntin' 'round to get it. You can find it here and there. Bring me a couple sea spices - two should do me for a while. And for my wife, bring me a ruby. A real one, no fakes. Then I'll pass you the sword. It's a nice one, let me tell ya!
AssignQuestStableKey: quest:a magical sword in port azure

DialogText: Sorry, not in a chattin' mood right now.
Oi, what brings an adventurer out this way? Not much to be had here anymore except trouble.
Lots of trouble! There's Molarai in the old beach fort, the beach is swarmin' with crocs, and those giant ghosts in the ruins... no thank you!
If you've travelled this far, you just may be worth paying attention to. Going into the Loomingwood?
Sivakaya is a Fallen God, but one day she may rise again. We work towards that each and every day. Her warped followers are lost, but she may not be. Not yet.
Quite a place now, it is. We were able to create wards that keep the Sivakayans out. It's our last sanctuary until we can restore things again.
This Shrine belongs to Soluna. One day, she'll re-possess the statue and grace us again with her presence.
Soluna takes on many forms, for your world she chose that one. Her very existence is like that of the universe - shapeless, timeless, and all encompassing.
Welcome! This is my bank wagon. I can store anything you need. Even give it to your pals, by your request of course.
Not often we see a passerby. Stick to the roads through the forest. Those who stray will find trouble.
Not often we see a passerby. Stick to the roads through the forest. Those who stray will find trouble.
Not often we see a passerby. Stick to the roads through the forest. Those who stray will find trouble.

Ah, you're one of the sort who's in the market for trouble, aren't ya? Well, you could do me a favor over in Krakengard to the south of here, or you could head north and try your luck in Underspine Hollow.
If you go to Krakengard, bring me the tower and bunk keys if you find extras. I've always wanted to explore that place.
RepeatingQuestDialog: I'm all set now, thank you.

DialogText: There's a dwelling over in the Revival Plains called Underspine Hollow. Full of Ogres and they love their Ogrespice. Bring me a few leaves... let's say 4? That'll do me for a while.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Just the ogrespice, please.

DialogText: Port Azure's got its nose in everyone's business. Poke the Sivakayans and they poke back. That's why we're out here alone. That's not our fight. There's more to what's going on than you know.
Look around ya. Ruins everywhere. Port Azure folk weren't the first ones to this land. We're on someone else's turf now, and I'm not sure we're exactly welcome.
Yarren keeps prattling on about the Molorai and some conspiracy. Spends his nights on the beach over by Krakengard, says he's waiting for Eldoth to pass though.
Settlin' here seemed like a good idea before all the stirrings took place. The skull monument was no big deal, 'cept now folks are here tryin' to wake it up.
Sivakayan ruins, so I hear. Been in there myself, nothin' to see. Just empty space and a weird feelin'.
Can you believe it? Goodsoil... and these creepy things roaming around just behind us too! What is happening to this place?

Behind us there? That's where they say Fernalla fell - looks like some sort of necromancy at work now though, those druid corpses crawled out of the ground not too long ago. Say, if you're going to dispose of them...
Some of 'em got those neat little bead necklaces - I think I could sell those. Bring me them if you find them and I'll save ya a trip back to Azure to sell 'em.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Keep 'em coming

DialogText: I've stumbled upon something notable about The Willow in my studies of Fernallan Lore. Would you like me to share it with you?

The Willow is eveywhere, and the branches could be anywhere. However, I've learned that the Goblin tribes in the Fernallan Meadows might hold more clues! If you find anything, bring it back to me. One day we'll wield its branches...
I've stumbled upon something notable about The Willow in my studies of Fernallan Lore. Would you like me to share it with you?

The Willow is eveywhere, and the branches could be anywhere. However, I've learned that the Rottenfoot tribes in the Fernallan Meadows might hold more clues! If you find anything, bring it back to me. One day we'll wield its branches...
... *it hums steadily*
Soluna... *its humming intensifies momentarily*
... *its humming pauses briefly then resumes*
... *it hums steadily*
Fernalla... *its humming intensifies momentarily*
... *its humming pauses briefly then resumes*
... *it hums steadily*
Vitheo... *its humming intensifies momentarily*
... *its humming pauses briefly then resumes*
We've uncovered a tomb. We believe it to be the resting place of Vitheo's wordly form. Our expeditions have not been successful, so maybe you can help us.

I believe the artifacts we could extract are worth the risks of bringing in strangers like you. We've sent scouts out into Erenshor looking for capable warriors to send to us. One of them must have taken a liking to you.
We've uncovered a tomb. We believe it to be the resting place of Vitheo's wordly form. Our expeditions have not been successful, so maybe you can help us.

I believe the artifacts we could extract are worth the risks of bringing in strangers like you. We've sent scouts out into Erenshor looking for capable warriors to send to us. One of them must have taken a liking to you.
You want to have a go at it? I've got an extra key. I'll trade ya. I'm a collector of rarities, after all. How about a Sivakrux?
You haven't run into one yet? Folks find them every now and again in the oddest places. Belly of a wolf, clutched by a skeleton, in the folds of a Giant... could be anywhere.
It was awful... they came from everywhere... I bet it was those apologists from Restful Valley... their houses aren't burned. Why do you think that is?
Sivakayans... it had to be. Armor as black as night...
Yarren? Haven't seen him since the raid. I hope he's ok.
Went and formed their own little town. Said that Port Azure has no right to spread out like this. They said the Sivakayans aren't that bad - hah! Look at our town!
There are no ships due in today. Or tomorrow. Next one is from Merosavilla, with reinforcements. We're not sure when.
Long ways off, it seems like. Where home used to be for a lot of these folks.
Ports are closed. Anyone headed this way gets dropped and left on Stowaway's Step.
Armor for sale. Take it or leave it.
Armor for sale. Take it or leave it.
If your journey here was made with intent to fight the Sivakayans, then you are welcome.
They do not represent what Sivakaya truly is. They are a product of corruption brought by Azynthi. Sivakaya's mind can be restored!
Woe is me! I must have a Moongill! I crave a Moongill! But the shop has none for sale and I don't know how to fish!
RepeatingQuestDialog: You've been handsomly rewarded!

DialogText: I need someone who knows how to fish to catch me one! There's an old fishing pole for sale in there, but who has time for that? To make it worse, they only come out at night! I'll give you a fancy reward if you catch me one...
RepeatingQuestDialog: I love Moongills! I can't wait to eat this.

DialogText: You stop with that nonsense, I have people like you to do things for me!
Only the worthy may pass.
Azynthi...
RepeatingQuestDialog: Azynthi...

DialogText: Only the worthy may pass.
Azynthi...
RepeatingQuestDialog: Azynthi...

DialogText: Only the worthy may pass.
Azynthi...

Azynthi...

DialogText: You may pass.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Azynthi...

DialogText: Do not pass unless you are deemed worthy. You will die.
The Loomingwood is no place to wander. Our wards protect this place from Sivakayans, but they attract what some might consider to be... far worse.
Beyond the road, things which are attracted to our magic wander beneath the canopy, but they do not venture close. We do not fare well away from the path.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Go to the boats and we'll leave.

DialogText: The willows... *Her eyes are locked on the distant willow's treetop"
... *the light dims briefly, and you hear a soft sign from within."
... *it does not speak*
... *the light dims briefly, and you hear a soft sign from within."
... *it does not speak*
... *the light dims briefly, and you hear a soft sign from within."
... *it does not speak*
... *the light dims briefly, and you hear a soft sign from within."
... *It is a Riftstone, willed into existence*
It keeps things out. It keeps things in. Hard to tell which side we're on in this light.
In this light, it's a nice quiet place. Don't you think so?
Looks like I'm stranded here for now. Might as well open up for business, I figure! Got any banking to do?
Those undead just keep pouring out of that place. They're not hard to dispatch, the one's we're seeing at least. What if something bigger comes out though?
They call it The Bonepits. It's the fastest way to the Hidden Hills settlement. We pass through now and again for supplies. I've got half a mind to raid that place and clear it out for good.
You take that job, that's on you. I imagine somewhere deep down in there is a... a leader? I don't even know what you'll find. Heard rumors of a giant demon who glows red though.
Bring me proof of whatever it is you slay. I've got somethin' here for ya if you do.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thanks for taking care of that.

DialogText: Ah, welcome to the settlement. Looking for a way to test your skills?
There's an old bear in the Brake - Grizzlepaw. Wedged in his teeth, there is an old chain from a pendant. Bring it to me, and I'll pay handsomely.
RepeatingQuestDialog: Thanks for finding that chain

DialogText: Belonged to a friend of mine, I suspect. Haven't seen it myself, but have heard enough tales to believe it's true.
Welcome to our little town. We're calling it Restful Valley. There's no trouble here so don't you go bringing any.
Everywhere but our little corner there's brigands and raiders and other beasts. That's not the life we want. We're peaceful, and we hope to be treated the same! Erenshor is everyone's home.
He passes through every now and again. Says the folks at Goodsoil are part of the problem.
This town holds secrets, lots of 'em. The kind that are best not found. Stay out of the shadows. That's my advice.
There's dark corners here. All around. Under your feet, even. Seen 'em with my own eyes. Lucky, I guess, that I'm still here. If you listen close in the right places, you can hear... their presence.
It is hopeless.
Our way of life ended with Soluna's impact. The materials flung into the air rained directly upon our home. We were a brotherhood of historians, and now we are locked forever in history.

Our skin turned to stone, our mortality was removed by the magic, and my brothers were driven to insanity by the years of unchanging pain. Soluna did not save us. Brax did not save us. Azynthi was our last hope, so we ventured to the spire to find him. He did not answer our calls.

Our cure likely does not exist. A softening spell is unheard of. It would need to be created somehow. Without it, there is no sense in returning home. Being away, we seem to have at least maintained our sanity.
Well met, I'm Xander, and I'm a bounty hunter. We're here to work, so if you don't have info or you're lookin' to trade, stand aside.

Lookin' for a missing person. I suspect the Molorai have him over in the Krakengard prison. He's apparently in a line of work that's right up their alley. The prison is hidden... but they say that you can hear the wind blow through it when you're close.

They're a group that took interest in Erenshor early on. They do call this place the Land of the Gods, afterall. Most all religious writings refer to this place. Old Eldoth Molorai's interested in that sort of thing.
I do my best to hold it all together here. The builders mostly ran back to Port Azure when the guards pulled back. Only a few of us remain now.
I do my best to hold it all together here. The builders mostly ran back to Port Azure when the guards pulled back. Only a few of us remain now.
You're not who I'm looking for.
Eldoth.
Have you seen him? It's imperative that I know where he is.
Brice...? What about him? Did he give you a message for me?
If you find a way off this rock... good for you. *he leans in and whispers* There's rumors of sailors who can get you to the mainland.
This place has nothing to offer. Erenshor is where you want to go. You can be anything there...
It's been too long since I've felt the pain of battle. The tomb down the hill calls to me.
